response:
To translate the Morse code, I'll break it down:

-.. = L
-. = O
--- = P
-.. = L
..-.--. = E M (Note: M is represented by a dash (-) in Morse code, and it's also the last two characters of the message, so I'll leave it as is)
--- = P
.-. = A
.- = O
..- = S
... = T
-.-.. = W
. = S (Note: The period at the end is a punctuation mark, not part of the message)
... = T

So, the Morse code translates to: L O P L E M P A O S T W S T
